title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: alignment system ramen quantifies similar two microbial communities functional perspective – species present, metabolite--metabolite pathways catalyse. result ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment (CMA) object containing similarity scores, pathway correspondences, (multiple alignments) consensus network. vignette covers alignment system depth. general introduction package, see vignette("ramen", package = "ramen").
code: library(ramen)

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: build six consortia bundled misosoup24 dataset use throughout vignette.
code: data("misosoup24") cm_list <- lapply(seq_len(6), function(i) {     ConsortiumMetabolism(         misosoup24[[i]],         name = names(misosoup24)[i],         species_col = "species",         metabolite_col = "metabolites",         flux_col = "fluxes"     ) })  cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(cm_list, name = "Demo")

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: align(CM, CM) compares two ConsortiumMetabolism objects returns CMA Type = "pairwise".
code: cma <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]]) cma #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment #> Name: NA #> Type: "pairwise" #> Metric: "FOS" #> Score: 0.7634 #> Query: "ac_A1R12_1", Reference: "ac_A1R12_10"

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Five metrics available via method argument. Regardless selected primary score, applicable metrics always computed stored. Metabolic Alignment Aggregate Score (MAAS) combines four metrics. Weights renormalized metrics unavailable (e.g., unweighted networks lack Bray-Curtis):
code: cma_fos <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]], method = "FOS") cma_jac <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]], method = "jaccard") scores(cma_fos) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129 cma_maas <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]], method = "MAAS") scores(cma_maas) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: alignment classifies every metabolite--metabolite pathway shared, unique query, unique reference.
code: ## All pathways in the alignment head(pathways(cma)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 2 #>   consumed produced #>   <chr>    <chr>    #> 1 acald    ac       #> 2 asp__L   ac       #> 3 gly      ac       #> 4 gthrd    ac       #> 5 h2o2     ac       #> 6 h2s      ac  ## Shared pathways only shared <- pathways(cma, type = "shared") nrow(shared) #> [1] 71  ## Unique pathways (returns a list with $query and $reference) unique_pw <- pathways(cma, type = "unique") nrow(unique_pw$query) #> [1] 22 nrow(unique_pw$reference) #> [1] 116

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Statistical significance assessed degree-preserving network rewiring. query network’s pathways shuffled preserving metabolite’s degree, metric recomputed null distribution. MAAS, binary network topology permuted; weighted assays (Consumption, Production, nSpecies) remain fixed, null distribution reflects topological variation composite score.
code: cma_p <- align(     cm_list[[1]],     cm_list[[2]],     method = "FOS",     computePvalue = TRUE,     nPermutations = 99L ) scores(cma_p) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: network view shows shared (green), query-unique (blue), reference-unique (red) pathways directed metabolite flow graph. Pairwise alignment network.
code: plot(cma, type = "network")

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Pairwise metric scores.
code: plot(cma, type = "scores")

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: align(CMS) computes pairwise similarities across consortia ConsortiumMetabolismSet returns CMA Type = "multiple".
code: cma_mult <- align(cms) cma_mult

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: similarity matrix n x n symmetric matrix 1s diagonal. FOS, derived pre-computed CMS overlap matrix:

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: multiple alignment, primary score median pairwise scores. scores() returns full summary statistics:

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Pathway prevalence counts many consortia share metabolite--metabolite pathway. enables classification pathways core (present consortia) niche (present ). pathways() method type = "consensus" returns information:
code: prev <- prevalence(cma_mult) head(prev[order(-prev$nConsortia), ]) #>    consumed produced nConsortia proportion #> 21   asp__L       ac          6          1 #> 24      gly       ac          6          1 #> 30      pyr       ac          6          1 #> 56   asp__L   ala__D          6          1 #> 59      gly   ala__D          6          1 #> 65      pyr   ala__D          6          1  ## Distribution of prevalence table(prev$nConsortia) #>  #>   1   2   3   4   5   6  #> 100  75  55  51  14  30 head(pathways(cma_mult, type = "consensus")) #>   consumed produced nConsortia proportion #> 1    acald    4abut          1  0.1666667 #> 2   arg__L    4abut          1  0.1666667 #> 3   asp__L    4abut          1  0.1666667 #> 4     etoh    4abut          1  0.1666667 #> 5      gly    4abut          1  0.1666667 #> 6   leu__L    4abut          1  0.1666667

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: heatmap shows pairwise similarities dendrogram-based ordering: Similarity heatmap.
code: plot(cma_mult, type = "heatmap")

title: Alignment of Microbial Consortia
text: Multiple alignment summary scores.
code: plot(cma_mult, type = "scores") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`). #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_text()`).

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen can installed Bioconductor:
code: if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))     install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install("ramen") library(ramen)

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen (Reconstruction Alignment Microbial Exchange Networks) analyses functional similarity microbial communities metabolic network perspective. Rather comparing communities species composition, ramen asks: metabolite--metabolite pathways community catalyse, similar resulting networks? package provides three S4 classes build Bioconductor’s TreeSummarizedExperiment: TreeSummarizedExperiment: ConsortiumMetabolism (CM) – single community’s metabolic exchange network ConsortiumMetabolismSet (CMS) – collection CMs precomputed overlap scores dendrogram ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment (CMA) – result aligning two communities vignette walks complete analysis data import alignment using bundled misosoup24 dataset (56 metabolic solutions MiSoSoup). details alignment metrics, see vignette("alignment", package = "ramen"); gallery plot types, see vignette("visualisation", package = "ramen").

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ramen ships misosoup24, list 56 metabolic solutions. element data.frame columns metabolites, species, fluxes, negative fluxes indicate consumption positive fluxes indicate production.
code: data("misosoup24") length(misosoup24) #> [1] 56 names(misosoup24)[1:8] #> [1] "ac_A1R12_1"  "ac_A1R12_10" "ac_A1R12_11" "ac_A1R12_12" "ac_A1R12_13" #> [6] "ac_A1R12_14" "ac_A1R12_15" "ac_A1R12_16" head(misosoup24[[1]]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   metabolites species  fluxes #>   <chr>       <chr>     <dbl> #> 1 ac          A1R12     0.773 #> 2 ac          I2R16   -10.8   #> 3 acald       A1R12    -1.12  #> 4 acald       I2R16     1.12  #> 5 ala__D      A1R12     0.760 #> 6 ala__D      I2R16    -0.760

title: Introduction to ramen
text: raw MiSoSoup output (nested YAML), importMisosoup() parses data structured tibbles consortia, media, growth information. overviewMisosoup() provides quick summary data structure full import.
code: ## Parse raw MiSoSoup YAML (not run -- requires external data) raw <- yaml::read_yaml("path/to/misosoup_output.yaml") overviewMisosoup(raw) result <- importMisosoup(raw) names(result) # "consortia", "media", "growth"

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ConsortiumMetabolism() constructor accepts data.frame columns species, metabolite, flux. Custom column names specified via species_col, metabolite_col, flux_col. data wide format separate columns consumed produced metabolites, pivotCM() converts long format ConsortiumMetabolism() expects:
code: wide_data <- data.frame(     species = c("Sp_A", "Sp_B", "Sp_C"),     uptake = c("met1", "met2", "met3"),     secretion = c("met2", "met3", "met1"),     flux = c(1, 1, 1) )  long_data <- pivotCM(     wide_data,     species = "species",     from = "uptake",     to = "secretion",     flux = "flux" ) head(long_data) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   species met    flux #>   <chr>   <chr> <dbl> #> 1 Sp_A    met1     -1 #> 2 Sp_A    met2      1 #> 3 Sp_B    met2     -1 #> 4 Sp_B    met3      1 #> 5 Sp_C    met3     -1 #> 6 Sp_C    met1      1

title: Introduction to ramen
code: cm1 <- ConsortiumMetabolism(     misosoup24[[1]],     name = names(misosoup24)[1],     species_col = "species",     metabolite_col = "metabolites",     flux_col = "fluxes" ) cm1 #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "ac_A1R12_1" #> Weighted metabolic network with 14 metabolites.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: quick testing, synCM() generates random consortium:
code: cm_syn <- synCM("Synthetic", n_species = 4, max_met = 8, seed = 42) cm_syn #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism #> Name: "Synthetic" #> Weighted metabolic network with 8 metabolites.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: CM inherits TreeSummarizedExperiment, standard dimension accessors work. Rows columns correspond metabolites (square metabolite--metabolite matrix): six assay matrices encode different views network:
code: dim(cm1) #> [1] 14 14 SummarizedExperiment::assayNames(cm1) #> [1] "Binary"               "nSpecies"             "Consumption"          #> [4] "Production"           "EffectiveConsumption" "EffectiveProduction"

title: Introduction to ramen
text: pathways() returns per-pathway summary statistics: consortia() returns tidy input data: Replacement methods allow renaming:
code: name(cm1) #> [1] "ac_A1R12_1" species(cm1) #> [1] "A1R12" "I2R16" metabolites(cm1) #>  [1] "ac"     "acald"  "ala__D" "ala__L" "asp__L" "co2"    "etoh"   "glu__L" #>  [9] "gly"    "gthox"  "gthrd"  "h2o2"   "h2s"    "pyr" head(pathways(cm1)) #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   consumed produced n_species #>   <chr>    <chr>        <dbl> #> 1 ac       acald            1 #> 2 ac       asp__L           1 #> 3 ac       co2              1 #> 4 ac       etoh             1 #> 5 ac       gly              1 #> 6 ac       gthrd            1 head(consortia(cm1)) #>      met species        flux #> 1     ac   A1R12   0.7729250 #> 2     ac   I2R16 -10.7673345 #> 3  acald   A1R12  -1.1209639 #> 4  acald   I2R16   1.1209639 #> 5 ala__D   A1R12   0.7603136 #> 6 ala__D   I2R16  -0.7603136 name(cm1) <- "first_solution" name(cm1) #> [1] "first_solution"

title: Introduction to ramen
text: ConsortiumMetabolismSet groups multiple CMs. construction, ramen automatically expands binary matrices shared universal metabolite space, computes pairwise overlap scores, builds hierarchical clustering dendrogram.
code: ## Build 20 CMs from misosoup24 cm_list <- lapply(seq_len(20), function(i) {     ConsortiumMetabolism(         misosoup24[[i]],         name = names(misosoup24)[i],         species_col = "species",         metabolite_col = "metabolites",         flux_col = "fluxes"     ) })  cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(cm_list, name = "MiSoSoup_20") cms

title: Introduction to ramen
text: species() returns species number distinct pathways catalyses: Filter metabolic role – generalists (top fraction pathway count) specialists (bottom fraction):
code: species(cms) #> # A tibble: 23 × 2 #>    species n_pathways #>    <chr>        <int> #>  1 A1R12          302 #>  2 D2M19          132 #>  3 E3M18          108 #>  4 F3R08           99 #>  5 m_3C02          96 #>  6 C2R02           91 #>  7 m_3D05          91 #>  8 C3R12           88 #>  9 I3M07           88 #> 10 B3M02           88 #> # ℹ 13 more rows species(cms, type = "generalists") #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   species n_pathways #>   <chr>        <int> #> 1 A1R12          302 #> 2 D2M19          132 #> 3 E3M18          108 #> 4 F3R08           99 species(cms, type = "specialists") #> # A tibble: 4 × 2 #>   species n_pathways #>   <chr>        <int> #> 1 B3R10           50 #> 2 I2R16           45 #> 3 A3R04           36 #> 4 C1M14           35

title: Introduction to ramen
text: pathways() type argument classifies pathways prevalence: quantileCutoff parameter controls threshold (default 0.1).
code: ## Pan-consortia: present in many consortia (top fraction) head(pathways(cms, type = "pan-cons")) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   consumed produced n_species n_cons #>   <chr>    <chr>        <int>  <int> #> 1 ac       pyr             20     20 #> 2 ala__L   pyr             20     20 #> 3 etoh     co2              5     20 #> 4 h2s      co2             15     20 #> 5 pyr      ac               1     20 #> 6 pyr      ala__L           1     20  ## Niche: present in few consortia (bottom fraction) head(pathways(cms, type = "niche")) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   consumed     produced n_species n_cons #>   <chr>        <chr>        <int>  <int> #> 1 LalaDgluMdap ac               1      1 #> 2 LalaDgluMdap ala__D           1      1 #> 3 LalaDgluMdap ala__L           1      1 #> 4 LalaDgluMdap co2              1      1 #> 5 LalaDgluMdap etoh             1      1 #> 6 LalaDgluMdap gthox            1      1

title: Introduction to ramen
text: functionalGroups() clusters species Jaccard similarity pathway sets:
code: fg <- functionalGroups(cms, k = 3) #> Loading required namespace: colorspace #> Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0. #> ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead. #> ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the dendextend package. #>   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/talgalili/dendextend/issues>. #> This warning is displayed once per session. #> Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was #> generated.

title: Introduction to ramen
text: CMS dendrogram numbered nodes. numbered internal nodes can used extract sub-clusters:
code: plot(cms) sub_cms <- extractCluster(cms, node_id = 1) sub_cms

title: Introduction to ramen
text: align() compares two CMs returns ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment: similarity metrics computed automatically: Pathway correspondences show shared unique pathways:
code: cma_pair <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]]) cma_pair #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment #> Name: NA #> Type: "pairwise" #> Metric: "FOS" #> Score: 0.7634 #> Query: "ac_A1R12_1", Reference: "ac_A1R12_10" scores(cma_pair) #> $FOS #> [1] 0.7634409 #>  #> $jaccard #> [1] 0.3397129 #>  #> $brayCurtis #> [1] 0.3115158 #>  #> $redundancyOverlap #> [1] 0.3397129 shared <- pathways(cma_pair, type = "shared") unique_pw <- pathways(cma_pair, type = "unique") nrow(shared) #> [1] 71 nrow(unique_pw$query) #> [1] 22 nrow(unique_pw$reference) #> [1] 116

title: Introduction to ramen
text: align() CMS computes pairwise similarities: similarity matrix summary scores: Pathway prevalence across consortia: detailed treatment alignment metrics, p-values, accessor functions, see vignette("alignment", package = "ramen").

title: Introduction to ramen
text: class plot() method. one example per class; full gallery, see vignette("visualisation", package = "ramen"). Metabolic network first consortium. Dendrogram 20 consortia. Pairwise similarity heatmap.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "Binary") plot(cms) plot(cma_mult, type = "heatmap")

title: Visualisation
text: ramen provides plot() methods three core classes: ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment. vignette visual gallery every plot type. context underlying analyses, see vignette("ramen", package = "ramen") vignette("alignment", package = "ramen").
code: library(ramen)

title: Visualisation
code: data("misosoup24") cm_list <- lapply(seq_len(6), function(i) {     ConsortiumMetabolism(         misosoup24[[i]],         name = names(misosoup24)[i],         species_col = "species",         metabolite_col = "metabolites",         flux_col = "fluxes"     ) })  cms <- ConsortiumMetabolismSet(cm_list, name = "Demo") cma_pair <- align(cm_list[[1]], cm_list[[2]]) cma_mult <- align(cms)

title: Visualisation
text: plot(CM) renders directed metabolic flow network using igraph. Nodes coloured role: lightblue sources (outgoing edges), salmon sinks (incoming edges), lightgoldenrod intermediate nodes (incoming outgoing). type argument selects assay matrix determines edge weights.

title: Visualisation
text: default view shows network structure uniform edge weights. Binary metabolic network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "Binary")

title: Visualisation
text: Edge weights reflect many species catalyse pathway. nSpecies-weighted network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "nSpecies")

title: Visualisation
text: Edges weighted summed flux magnitudes. Consumption-weighted network. Production-weighted network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "Consumption") plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "Production")

title: Visualisation
text: Effective diversity measures evenly species contribute pathway (Shannon-based effective number species). Effective consumption network. Effective production network.
code: plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "EffectiveConsumption") plot(cm_list[[1]], type = "EffectiveProduction")

title: Visualisation
text: plot(CMS) draws hierarchical clustering dendrogram numbered internal nodes. node IDs used extractCluster() pull sub-clusters. CMS dendrogram numbered nodes.
code: plot(cms)

title: Visualisation
text: Supply data.frame mapping leaf labels colours. CMS dendrogram coloured labels.
code: colour_map <- data.frame(     label = names(misosoup24)[1:6],     colour = c(         "steelblue", "firebrick", "forestgreen",         "darkorange", "purple", "darkred"     ) ) plot(cms, label_colours = colour_map)

title: Visualisation
text: CMA plot() method supports three types: "heatmap", "network", "scores". default "network" pairwise "heatmap" multiple alignments.

title: Visualisation
text: Pairwise FOS similarities dendrogram-based ordering. Values range 0 (overlap) 1 (identical). Similarity heatmap.
code: plot(cma_mult, type = "heatmap")

title: Visualisation
text: Shared pathways green, query-unique blue, reference-unique red. Pairwise alignment network.
code: plot(cma_pair, type = "network")

title: Visualisation
text: bar chart displays similarity metrics pairwise alignments summary statistics multiple alignments. Pairwise alignment scores. Multiple alignment summary scores.
code: plot(cma_pair, type = "scores") plot(cma_mult, type = "scores") #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_col()`). #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_text()`).

title: Visualisation
text: fine-grained control network layout, call plotDirectedFlow() directly igraph object. function underlying CM CMA network plots. Custom directed flow plot.
code: g <- igraph::graph_from_adjacency_matrix(     SummarizedExperiment::assay(cm_list[[2]], "nSpecies"),     mode = "directed",     weighted = TRUE )  plotDirectedFlow(     g,     color_edges_by_weight = TRUE,     edge_width_range = c(0.5, 2),     vertex_size = 12,     vertex_label_cex = 0.7,     main = paste0(name(cm_list[[2]]), " - nSpecies (custom)") )

title: Reconstruction and Alignment of Microbial Exchange Networks
text: Bioconductor style guide - Classes: UpperCamelCase - Methods functions: lowerCamelCase

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
text: Creates ConsortiumMetabolism object representing metabolic interactions microbial community. object stores metabolite consumption production different species, along flux sums effective fluxes.

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
code: ConsortiumMetabolism(   data,   name = NA_character_,   species_col = "species",   metabolite_col = "met",   flux_col = "flux",   ... )

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
text: data data.frame columns species, metabolites fluxes. Fluxes can weighted unweighted (magnitude 1). name Character scalar giving consortium name. species_col Character scalar species column name, defaults "species". metabolite_col Character scalar metabolite column name, defaults "met". flux_col Character scalar flux column name, defaults "flux". ... Additional arguments passed constructor.

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
text: ConsortiumMetabolism object.

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
text: Name character. Display name consortium. Description character. Optional short description. Pathways data.frame. Pathway list metabolic interactions per-pathway metrics (species, flux sums, effective diversity). Weighted logical. Whether flux magnitudes used. InputData data.frame. Original input data (species, metabolite, flux columns). Metabolites character. Unique metabolite identifiers. Graphs list. List containing igraph object metabolic network.

title: Functional Microbiome Representation — ConsortiumMetabolism-class
code: cm <- synCM("example", n_species = 3, max_met = 5) cm #>  #> ── ConsortiumMetabolism  #> Name: "example" #> Weighted metabolic network with 3 metabolites.

title: Constructor for ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment Objects — ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment-class
text: Builds valid ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment constructing TSE base setting CMA-specific slots sensible defaults.

title: Constructor for ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment Objects — ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment-class
code: ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment(..., tse = NULL)

title: Constructor for ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment Objects — ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment-class
text: ... Named CMA slot values set (e.g., Type = "pairwise", PrimaryScore = 0.8). tse optional TreeSummarizedExperiment use base. Default creates empty TSE.

title: Constructor for ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment Objects — ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment-class
text: validated ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object.

title: Constructor for ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment Objects — ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment-class
text: Name character. Display name alignment. Description character. Optional short description. Type character. One "pairwise", "multiple", "search". Metric character. Similarity metric used (e.g., "FOS"). Params list. Additional parameters passed alignment method. QueryName character. Name query consortium (pairwise). ReferenceName character. Name reference consortium (pairwise). Scores list. Named list computed score components. PrimaryScore numeric. Primary similarity score, 0 1. Pvalue numeric. Permutation p-value, 0 1. SharedPathways data.frame. Pathways present query reference. UniqueQuery data.frame. Pathways unique query. UniqueReference data.frame. Pathways unique reference. SimilarityMatrix matrix. Pairwise similarity matrix (multiple alignment). ConsensusPathways data.frame. Consensus network pathways (multiple alignment). Prevalence data.frame. Pathway prevalence across consortia (multiple alignment). Dendrogram list. Hierarchical clustering dendrogram (multiple alignment). Pathways data.frame. Combined pathway list. Graphs list. List igraph objects. Metabolites data.frame. Metabolite mapping table.

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways
text: Retrieves pathways representing metabolic interactions species. default, returns concise summary columns consumed, produced, n_species. ConsortiumMetabolismSet objects, n_cons included well. Set verbose = TRUE return full pathway data including flux statistics, indices, per-species detail. argument type can used return specific types pathways ConsortiumMetabolismSet object: "" returns pathways "pan-cons" returns pathways present consortia "niche" returns niche pathways specific consortia "core" returns core metabolic pathways shared across species "aux" returns auxiliary pathways found species ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment objects, type selects pathway subset: "" returns union pathways "shared" (pairwise ) returns pathways shared query reference "unique" (pairwise ) returns pathways unique query reference list "consensus" (multiple ) returns consensus network pathways prevalence

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways
code: pathways(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolism' pathways(object, verbose = FALSE)  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment' pathways(   object,   type = c("all", "shared", "unique", "consensus"),   verbose = FALSE )  # S4 method for class 'ConsortiumMetabolismSet' pathways(   object,   type = c("all", "pan-cons", "niche", "core", "aux"),   quantileCutoff = 0.1,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways
text: object ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment object. ... Object specific arguments. See methods details. verbose Logical scalar. FALSE (default), returns concise summary columns consumed, produced, n_species. TRUE, returns full pathway data including flux statistics, effective values, per-species detail. type Character scalar giving type pathways output. quantileCutoff Numeric scalar 0 1 giving quantile threshold use filtering pathways. "pan-cons" "core" types, pathways 1 - quantileCutoff returned. "niche" "aux" types, pathways quantileCutoff returned. Defaults 0.1 (.e., top/bottom 10 percent).

title: Retrieve Metabolic Pathways — pathways

title: ramen 0.0.0.9001
text: Initial development release. Three core S4 classes: ConsortiumMetabolism, ConsortiumMetabolismSet, ConsortiumMetabolismAlignment, inheriting TreeSummarizedExperiment. Import microbial metabolic network data MiSoSoup YAML format via importMisosoup(). Pairwise multiple alignment consortium metabolisms using functional overlap scores (FOS), Jaccard, Bray-Curtis, redundancy overlap metrics. Visualization methods including heatmaps, network plots, alignment score plots. Functional group analysis hierarchical clustering species shared metabolic pathways.
